subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Cards
- **15 character cards** — The full influence deck.
- **3 Duke cards** — Character role copies.
- **3 Assassin cards** — Character role copies.
- **3 Contessa cards** — Character role copies.
- **3 Captain cards** — Character role copies.
- **3 Ambassador cards** — Character role copies in base game.

#### Currency
- **50 coins** — Shared treasury supply.

#### Reference
- **Summary cards** — Turn/action reminder aids.

#### Optional Variant Component
- **Inquisitor role option** — Replaces Ambassador when using the optional rule variant.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Steps
1. **Shuffle deck** — Shuffle all 15 character cards together.
2. **Deal influence** — Deal 2 face-down cards to each player.
3. **Deal coins** — Give each player exactly 2 coins.
4. **Create Court deck** — Put all undealt character cards face-down as the Court deck.
5. **Create treasury** — Put remaining coins in a shared treasury pile.

#### Hidden Information
- **Influence secrecy** — Keep your 2 influence cards face-down and hidden from opponents.
- **Active influence** — Face-down influence is usable influence.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Detected count rule** — Official rules state game supports 2–6 players.
- **Detected setup scaling** — No different setup numbers are listed by specific player count.
- **Detected action scaling** — No action changes are listed by specific player count.
- **Detected scoring scaling** — No scoring changes are listed by specific player count.
- **Detected solo mode** — No solo mode rules are listed.
- **Detected variant** — Optional Inquisitor variant replaces Ambassador role behavior.

#### Count Confirmation
--- 2 Players ---
- **Support status** — Officially supported.
- **Setup difference** — No count-specific setup changes listed.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Support status** — Officially supported.
- **Setup difference** — No count-specific setup changes listed.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Support status** — Officially supported.
- **Setup difference** — No count-specific setup changes listed.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Support status** — Officially supported.
- **Setup difference** — No count-specific setup changes listed.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Support status** — Officially supported.
- **Setup difference** — No count-specific setup changes listed.

#### First Turn
- **Start player** — Choose first player by agreed method.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.
- **Turn choice** — On your turn, take exactly one action declaration.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Mandatory coup check** — If you have 10+ coins, you must choose Coup.
2. **Declare action** — Declare one basic action or one character action claim.
3. **Challenge window** — Opponents may challenge any claimed character action or character-based block.
4. **Resolve challenge** — Apply reveal/loss rules immediately if challenge occurs.
5. **Resolve action** — Complete action effect if legal and not successfully stopped.
6. **Apply influence loss** — Any player losing influence reveals one chosen face-down card.
7. **Pass turn** — Continue clockwise.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Basic Actions
- **Income** — Take 1 coin from treasury.
- **Income blockability** — Income is not blocked by character text in the provided rules.
- **Foreign Aid** — Take 2 coins from treasury.
- **Foreign Aid block** — Duke can block Foreign Aid.
- **Coup** — Pay 7 coins and force one target opponent to lose 1 influence.
- **Mandatory Coup threshold** — If you have 10 or more coins, Coup is required on your turn.

#### Character Action Claims
- **Duke action** — Take 3 coins from treasury.
- **Assassin action** — Pay 3 coins to attempt to assassinate one opponent influence.
- **Captain action** — Take 2 coins from another player (steal).
- **Ambassador action** — Draw 2 from Court deck, choose exchange with hidden influence, return 2 to Court deck.

#### Defensive Blocks
- **Duke block** — Block someone from taking Foreign Aid.
- **Contessa block** — Block an assassination attempt against yourself.
- **Captain block** — Block someone from stealing from you.
- **Ambassador block** — Block someone from stealing from you.

#### Claim Legality
- **Bluff permission** — You may declare any character action whether or not you actually hold that character.
- **Challenge risk** — Any declared character claim is challengeable.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Influence System
- **Influence definition** — Influence is represented by your face-down character cards.
- **Lose influence** — When required, reveal one of your face-down cards face-up.
- **Face-up status** — Face-up characters cannot be used for actions or blocks.
- **Elimination** — If both your cards are face-up, you are out of the game.

#### Challenge Resolution
- **Default success** — Character actions and character blocks succeed unless challenged.
- **Failed proof** — If challenged and you cannot reveal required character, you lose 1 influence.
- **Successful proof** — If challenged and you reveal required character, challenger loses 1 influence.
- **Reshuffle step** — A successfully revealed character is shuffled into Court deck.
- **Redraw step** — After reshuffling, draw a replacement hidden character card.

#### Optional Inquisitor Variant
- **Role swap** — Inquisitor replaces Ambassador in optional rules.
- **Inquisitor option A** — Draw 1 from Court deck and choose whether to exchange with hidden influence.
- **Inquisitor option B** — Force an opponent to show one chosen hidden card (opponent chooses which card to show).
- **Forced redraw option** — Inquisitor may force that opponent card to be shuffled back and redrawn.
- **Steal defense** — Inquisitor blocks stealing.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Condition
- **Last influence standing** — Game ends when only one player has at least one face-down influence card.

#### Winner
- **Victory condition** — The last player with influence wins immediately.

#### Scoring Note
- **No VP track** — Coup has no point-scoring system in the provided rules.
- **No tiebreaker needed** — Survival state defines a single winner.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Table Positioning
- **Establish credibility** — Make early claims that can be defended consistently over multiple turns.
- **Track thresholds** — Watch who can reach 3 coins (assassin pressure) and 7 coins (coup pressure).
- **Control threat level** — Build coins without becoming the automatic table target.

#### Practical Opening Lines
- **Safe economy line** — Use Income/Foreign Aid cadence when challenge pressure is high.
- **Tempo bluff line** — Claim Duke early only if your table is challenge-shy or you can absorb a failed challenge.
- **Defense planning** — Keep at least one believable defensive claim online before opponents hit 3+ coins.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-challenging** — Challenging weakly-read claims burns influence faster than it gains control.
- **Missing forced coup** — Trying non-coup actions at 10+ coins is illegal and usually costs tempo.
- **Chaotic bluffing** — Switching claimed roles every turn makes you easy to call out.
- **Ignoring coin math** — Not tracking coin totals causes surprise assassinations and coups.
- **Wrong reveal choice** — Revealing the wrong card when losing influence can destroy your remaining bluff tree.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Economy Speed vs Table Heat
- **Fast money** — Higher coin gain gives faster pressure but paints a target.
- **Low-profile money** — Slower gain reduces threat perception but risks falling behind.

#### Decision 2: Challenge Now vs Challenge Later
- **Immediate challenge** — Stops momentum if correct but can lose influence if wrong.
- **Delayed challenge** — Preserves influence while collecting more read data.

#### Decision 3: Assassin Spend vs Coup Save
- **Assassin route** — Pay 3 for earlier elimination pressure and bluff leverage.
- **Coup route** — Save to 7 for guaranteed, unblockable influence removal.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for skimmable style with explicit player-count audit and exact count headers where applicable.
